title: Humanities annual updates 20-21.docx
text:
College of the Siskiyous
Humanities
Program Review Annual Update
2020-21
Faculty and staff providing input: Maria Fernandez, Michael Tischler, Chris Vancil
Principal Preparer: Valerie Roberts, Interim Dean of Liberal Arts and Student Success
This document is designed to be an update, however the full-time faculty teaching in the
Humanities disciplines report that a Comprehensive Program Review was not done for this area
when last due in 2018-19.
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
• Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, all classes in the Humanities area taught in person
were abruptly transitioned to remote instruction mid spring 2020 semester.
• Curriculum was updated to reflect the new course structure for English because of
AB 705 implementation – prerequisites for ENGL 0900 were removed. Humanities
area faculty have reported a need for a higher level of support for students in
reading and writing as a result.
• The Humanities disciplines (HUM and PHIL) FTES from 2018-19 to 2019-20 are as
follows:
2018-19 2019-20
HUM 22.33 28.68
PHIL 11.87 11.12
• Students earned the following awards in the Humanities disciplines:
2019-20
Humanities AA 2
LAS Humanities AA 9
Philosophy AA 1
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
Course assignments and grades are being consistently evaluated for student
understanding and learning, however without an institutional process, faculty have not
officially been assessing SLOs or PLOs.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
Support
• The Distance Learning Coordinator provided support to faculty.

-- 1 of 2 --

Obstacles
• The elimination of the ENGL 0900 course, which was a prerequisite to HUM 1001, has
meant some students come in with a lower skillset.
• Moving all in-person classes to remote learning in the middle of the spring 2020
semester required a change in modality for both the faculty and the students. This was
difficult for many, and student participation decreased.
• Some faculty and many students did not have the technology needed to be as successful
with remote instruction/learning.
• Instructional space to accommodate multimedia is needed.
• Films on Demand does not have adequate or current material.
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications.
• Technology for faculty – has been addressed.
• Database with more current material.
CQIPs needed?
• None noted.

-- 2 of 2 --
